properties.name: Tohoku Earthquake & Tsunami
properties.description: The most powerful earthquake ever recorded in Japan triggered a massive tsunami with waves up to 40 meters. It caused the Fukushima nuclear disaster and widespread destruction across the northeast coast.

properties.name: Tohoku Earthquake & Tsunami
properties.description: Affected area spanning the northeast coast of Honshu.

properties.name: Haiti Earthquake
properties.description: Devastating earthquake that struck near Port-au-Prince, causing catastrophic damage to the capital. One of the deadliest earthquakes in recorded history.

properties.name: Kashmir Earthquake
properties.description: Massive earthquake centered in Pakistan-administered Kashmir, destroying entire towns and leaving millions homeless in mountainous terrain.

properties.name: San Francisco Earthquake
properties.description: Major earthquake and subsequent fires destroyed over 80% of San Francisco. One of the most significant natural disasters in US history.

properties.name: Tangshan Earthquake
properties.description: One of the deadliest earthquakes in modern history, striking an industrial city of one million people in the early morning hours while most were asleep.

properties.name: Nepal Earthquake
properties.description: Powerful earthquake that triggered avalanches on Mount Everest and destroyed historic temples in Kathmandu Valley.

properties.name: Turkey-Syria Earthquake
properties.description: Devastating doublet earthquakes struck southeastern Turkey and northern Syria, causing massive destruction across a wide area in winter conditions.

properties.name: Hurricane Katrina
properties.description: One of the deadliest hurricanes in US history. Storm surge breached levees in New Orleans, flooding 80% of the city. Caused $125 billion in damage.

properties.name: Hurricane Katrina
properties.description: Affected area across the Gulf Coast.

properties.name: Hurricane Harvey
properties.description: Record-breaking rainfall event. Harvey stalled over southeast Texas, dumping over 1.5 meters of rain on Houston over four days, causing catastrophic flooding.

properties.name: Typhoon Haiyan (Yolanda)
properties.description: One of the most powerful tropical cyclones ever recorded. Storm surge devastated Tacloban City and surrounding areas in the Philippines.

properties.name: Hurricane Dorian
properties.description: Stalled over the Bahamas for over 24 hours as a Category 5 hurricane, causing total devastation. Strongest Atlantic hurricane to make landfall on record.

properties.name: Hurricane Andrew
properties.description: Extremely powerful and destructive hurricane that struck south Florida, causing $27 billion in damage (1992 USD) and destroying thousands of homes.

properties.name: Australian Black Summer Bushfires
properties.region: New South Wales, Victoria, South Australia
properties.description: Unprecedented bushfire season burning over 46 million acres across southeastern Australia. Estimated 3 billion animals killed or displaced.

properties.name: Australian Black Summer Bushfires
properties.description: Burned area across southeastern Australia.

properties.name: Camp Fire
properties.description: Deadliest and most destructive wildfire in California history. The town of Paradise was almost entirely destroyed within hours.

properties.name: Western US Wildfire Season
properties.description: Record-breaking wildfire season across the western United States. Multiple megafires burned simultaneously, creating hazardous air quality across the region.

properties.name: Canadian Wildfires
properties.description: Record-breaking wildfire season in Canada with smoke blanketing much of North America. Over 6,500 fires burned across every province and territory.

properties.name: Dust Bowl
properties.description: Prolonged severe drought combined with decades of poor farming practices caused massive dust storms across the American Great Plains. Millions displaced during the Great Depression.

properties.name: East Africa Drought
properties.description: Severe drought across the Horn of Africa caused a massive humanitarian crisis. Famine was declared in parts of Somalia. Over 13 million people affected.

properties.name: India Drought
properties.description: Severe water crisis affected over 600 million people in India. Chennai nearly ran out of water. Delayed monsoon worsened already critical drought conditions.

properties.name: Pakistan Floods
properties.description: Devastating monsoon floods submerged approximately one-fifth of Pakistan's total land area. Over 20 million people affected, with massive destruction of infrastructure and agriculture.

properties.name: Pakistan Floods
properties.description: Catastrophic flooding caused by extreme monsoon rains submerged one-third of Pakistan. Over 33 million people displaced. Attributed to climate change intensifying monsoon patterns.

properties.name: Western Europe Floods
properties.description: Extreme rainfall caused devastating flash floods across western Germany and Belgium. The Ahr Valley was particularly hard hit, with entire villages destroyed.

properties.name: Yangtze River Floods
properties.description: Catastrophic flooding along the Yangtze River affected over 200 million people. The floods prompted the Chinese government to ban logging in the upper Yangtze watershed.

properties.name: Krakatoa Eruption
properties.description: Catastrophic eruption that destroyed most of the island. The explosion was heard 4,800 km away and triggered tsunamis up to 30 meters high. Caused global cooling of 1.2°C.

properties.name: Mount St. Helens Eruption
properties.description: Massive lateral eruption blew off the north face of the mountain. The eruption column reached 24 km. The blast zone extended 30 km, destroying 600 sq km of forest.

properties.name: Mount St. Helens Eruption
properties.description: Blast zone and ashfall area.

properties.name: Mount Pinatubo Eruption
properties.description: Second-largest volcanic eruption of the 20th century. Ejected 10 cubic km of material and caused global temperatures to drop by about 0.5°C for two years.

properties.name: Hunga Tonga-Hunga Ha'apai Eruption
properties.description: Massive underwater volcanic eruption that generated tsunamis across the Pacific. The eruption column reached 58 km, the highest ever recorded. Severed Tonga's undersea internet cable.

properties.name: Indian Ocean Tsunami
properties.description: Triggered by a 9.1 magnitude earthquake off the coast of Sumatra, this tsunami devastated coastlines across the Indian Ocean. Waves up to 30 meters struck 14 countries. One of the deadliest natural disasters in recorded history.

properties.name: Indian Ocean Tsunami
properties.description: Affected coastlines across the Indian Ocean.

properties.name: Tohoku Tsunami
properties.description: Massive tsunami triggered by the 9.1 Tohoku earthquake. Waves up to 40 meters devastated the northeast coast and caused the Fukushima nuclear disaster.

properties.name: Tri-State Tornado
properties.description: Deadliest single tornado in US history. Traveled 352 km across three states, maintaining contact with the ground for a record 3.5 hours.

properties.name: Joplin Tornado
properties.description: Deadliest tornado in the US since 1947. The massive EF5 tornado was nearly 1.6 km wide and devastated a third of the city of Joplin.

properties.name: Moore Tornado
properties.description: Devastating EF5 tornado struck Moore, Oklahoma for the third time in 14 years. The tornado was 2.1 km wide with winds up to 340 km/h.

properties.name: 2011 Super Outbreak
properties.description: Largest tornado outbreak ever recorded, spawning 362 tornadoes across the southeastern United States over three days. Four EF5 tornadoes confirmed.

properties.name: European Heatwave
properties.description: Record-breaking heatwave across Europe. France was hardest hit with nearly 15,000 excess deaths. Hottest European summer in at least 500 years at the time.

properties.name: Russian Heatwave
properties.description: Unprecedented heatwave and drought in western Russia. Record temperature of 40°C in Moscow. Massive wildfires blanketed the city in smoke. Significant crop failures.

properties.name: European Heatwave 2022
properties.description: Intense heatwave shattered records across Europe. UK recorded temperatures above 40°C for the first time. Widespread wildfires and infrastructure damage.

properties.name: India-Pakistan Heatwave
properties.description: Severe heatwave with temperatures reaching 48°C. India's fifth deadliest heatwave. Pakistan also severely affected with over 1,200 deaths in Karachi alone.

properties.name: North American Ice Storm
properties.description: Catastrophic ice storm that coated everything in up to 80mm of ice. Over 4 million people lost power, some for weeks. Caused $5.4 billion in damage.

properties.name: Texas Winter Storm Uri
properties.description: Severe winter storm brought unprecedented cold, ice, and snow to Texas. Power grid collapsed leaving millions without electricity and water for days in freezing temperatures.

properties.name: Storm of the Century
properties.description: Massive cyclonic blizzard stretching from Central America to eastern Canada. Produced record snowfall, hurricane-force winds, and spawned tornadoes in Florida.

properties.name: Northeastern United States Blizzard
properties.description: Catastrophic blizzard paralyzed the northeastern US for over a week. Over 70 cm of snow fell in some areas with hurricane-force winds creating massive drifts.

properties.name: Polar Vortex 2014
properties.description: Extreme polar vortex event brought Arctic air across North America. Wind chills reached -50°C in some areas. The Great Lakes nearly completely froze.

properties.name: European Cold Wave
properties.description: Severe cold wave across Europe with temperatures plunging to -33°C. Particularly devastating in Eastern Europe where many homeless people died.

properties.name: Great Chilean Earthquake
properties.description: Most powerful earthquake ever recorded. Triggered tsunamis that crossed the Pacific Ocean, causing deaths in Hawaii, Japan, and the Philippines.

properties.name: Hurricane Irma
properties.description: One of the strongest Atlantic hurricanes ever recorded. Maintained Category 5 intensity for a record 60 hours. Devastated Barbuda and the US Virgin Islands.

properties.name: Thailand Floods
properties.description: Worst flooding in Thailand in over 50 years. Over 65 of 77 provinces affected. Major industrial estates near Bangkok submerged, causing global supply chain disruptions.

properties.name: Sumatra-Andaman Earthquake
properties.region: Off the west coast of Sumatra
properties.description: Third-largest earthquake ever recorded. Ruptured over 1,300 km of fault line. Triggered the devastating Indian Ocean tsunami.

properties.name: Mount Tambora Eruption
properties.description: Largest volcanic eruption in recorded history. Caused the 'Year Without a Summer' in 1816, with global crop failures and famine.

properties.name: Southern Africa Drought
properties.description: El Niño-driven drought affected over 40 million people across southern Africa. Crop production fell by up to 50% in some countries. Major food security crisis.

properties.name: Peshtigo Fire
properties.description: Deadliest wildfire in American history, occurring the same night as the Great Chicago Fire. A firestorm destroyed the town of Peshtigo and surrounding areas.

properties.name: Lisbon Earthquake & Tsunami
properties.description: Massive earthquake followed by a tsunami and fires destroyed most of Lisbon. One of the deadliest earthquakes in history, fundamentally changing European philosophy and science.

properties.name: Galveston Hurricane
properties.description: Deadliest natural disaster in US history. The entire city of Galveston was destroyed by storm surge up to 4.6 meters. Led to construction of the Galveston Seawall.

properties.name: Mexico City Earthquake
properties.description: Devastating earthquake that struck Mexico City, causing widespread building collapse. The soft lakebed beneath the city amplified seismic waves, causing disproportionate destruction.

properties.name: China Floods
properties.description: The deadliest natural disaster in recorded history. Flooding along three major river systems caused by cyclones following a drought. Famine and disease followed the initial flooding.

properties.name: Afghanistan Blizzard
properties.description: Severe winter with heavy snowfall and extreme cold across Afghanistan. Remote villages were cut off for weeks. Thousands of livestock killed, devastating rural livelihoods.

properties.name: January 2018 Cold Wave
properties.description: Intense 'bomb cyclone' brought blizzard conditions and extreme cold to the eastern US and Canada. Temperatures in some areas dropped below -40°C.

properties.name: Eruption of Mount Vesuvius
properties.description: One of the most famous volcanic eruptions in history. Buried the Roman cities of Pompeii and Herculaneum under meters of volcanic ash and pumice.

properties.name: Shaanxi Earthquake
properties.description: Deadliest earthquake in recorded history. Many victims lived in yaodongs (cave dwellings) carved from loess cliffs which collapsed during the shaking.

properties.name: Cyclone Nargis
properties.description: Deadliest natural disaster in Myanmar's history. Storm surge of up to 5 meters devastated the low-lying Irrawaddy Delta. The military government initially blocked international aid.

properties.name: Black Saturday Bushfires
properties.description: Deadliest bushfire disaster in Australian history. Temperatures exceeded 46°C with winds up to 100 km/h. Over 2,000 homes destroyed across multiple fire fronts.

properties.name: Mumbai Floods
properties.description: Record-breaking rainfall of 944mm in 24 hours inundated Mumbai. The city was paralyzed for days. Highlighted severe infrastructure and drainage problems.

properties.name: Sichuan Earthquake
properties.description: Devastating earthquake that struck mountainous Sichuan province. Thousands of school buildings collapsed, sparking national outrage over construction quality. Over 5 million people made homeless.

properties.name: Cyclone Amphan
properties.description: Strongest cyclone to form in the Bay of Bengal since 1999. Caused extensive damage in Kolkata and coastal Bangladesh. Effective evacuations limited deaths.

properties.name: Cape Town Water Crisis
properties.description: Three-year drought brought Cape Town to the brink of becoming the first major city to run out of water. 'Day Zero' was predicted when taps would be turned off. Averted through severe water restrictions.

properties.name: 2013 Central US Ice Storm
properties.description: Major ice storm system impacted a broad swath from Texas to the Great Lakes. Over 500,000 customers lost power. Significant tree and infrastructure damage.
